title: Gas hydrate versus seabed morphology offshore Lebu (Chilean margin)
abstract: Seabed morphology growth related to fluid migration offshore Lebu (~38°S) is analyzed through stable water isotopes, bathymetric data, and marine seismic reflection profiles. The BSR detected in the seismic data indicates the transition between gas hydrate and free gas zones. The orientation of the mud cone relief is associated with fault-fracture pathways for fluid migration, and isotope data document past hydrate melting events.

location.description: Offshore Lebu (~38°S), central Chilean margin; gas hydrate and seabed morphology study

data.name: MCS Profiles — Offshore Lebu ~38°S
data.description: Marine seismic reflection profiles offshore Lebu (~38°S) showing BSR distribution and fluid migration pathways associated with mud cone seabed morphology

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — Offshore Lebu
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry showing mud cone morphology offshore Lebu associated with fluid migration

_data_note: Scientific Reports open access paper. Data repository for seismic data not confirmed; bathymetry may be at MGDS.

processing.summary: Marine seismic reflection profiles and bathymetric data offshore Lebu (~38°S) were analyzed to characterize BSR distribution and link seabed mud cone morphology to gas hydrate-related fluid migration. Stable water isotopes from sampling complement the seismic analysis. The BSR indicates the gas hydrate/free gas transition, and fault-fracture systems provide pathways for fluid migration creating the seafloor mud cone relief.
workflow: 4. Correlation of fault-fractures with BSR discontinuities

analysis_notes: Scientific Reports open access paper. Full text freely available at nature.com. Seismic data source not confirmed from available information.
